channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadians Adjusting Spending Habits Amid Rising Financial Pressures
item.description: A new report by MNP Ltd., based on Ipsos polling, reveals that Canadians are increasingly burdened by financial pressures that force them to cut back on non-essential expenses before receiving their p...

item.title: Chadema Geita chairperson faces court appearance after arrest
item.description: Neema Chozaire, the chairperson of Chadema Geita constituency, is set to appear in court on Tuesday following her arrest on June 29, 2026. She was taken into police custody after being arrested while ...

item.title: Tom Cruise's 'Digger' Trailer: Oil Baron's Mission to Save the World in Alejandro G. Inarritu's Comedy
item.description: The article discusses the release of the 'Digger' movie trailer, starring Tom Cruise as an oil baron attempting to save the world. Directed by Alejandro G. Inarritu, the film features a stellar cast i...

item.title: CBCP Launches Mental Health Initiatives with Counseling and Apologies
item.description: The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) has issued a pastoral letter addressing mental health awareness, emphasizing the importance of compassion, understanding, and professional ca...

item.title: Four Killed in Mpumalanga Car Shooting as Suspects Fire Over 40 Shots
item.description: A traumatic incident unfolded in Mkhuhlu, Bushbuckridge, Mpumalanga, where four people were gunned down while sitting inside a car after gunmen fired over 40 shots. Phumla Mona, an 18-year-old brother...

item.title: Senate investigates N34tn import duty waivers and threatens sanctions for non-compliant MDAs
item.description: The Senate Committee on Finance has launched an investigation into the Federal Government's issuance of Import Duty Exemption Certificates (IDEC) worth N34 trillion between 2020 and 2025, warning that...

item.title: Debt Dispute Escalates to Murder in Kenya
item.description: A Kenyan court has shifted a civil debt case involving 18,000 shillings into a murder charge after the dispute turned deadly. The original case centered on an outstanding debt of approximately $140, w...

item.title: Toronto Police Identify Victims in Salsa on St. Clair Shooting
item.description: Toronto police have identified the victims of a deadly shooting that occurred during the Salsa on St. Clair festival. The incident, which left two individuals dead, has sparked significant concern abo...

item.title: Junior Boks rally to defeat England and advance to U-20 World Championship final
item.description: The Junior Springboks delivered a stunning second-half performance to overcome England 53-37 in the U-20 World Championship semifinal in Tbilisi, Georgia. Trailing 20-12 at halftime, South Africa's un...

item.title: Unidentified Man Allegedly Assaults 69-Year-Old Woman at Dickson Home
item.description: A 69-year-old woman was allegedly assaulted by an unidentified man at her residence in Dickson, Canberra. The incident occurred on June 27, 2026, when the man demanded to speak to 'Valentino' before f...

item.title: President Marcos Praises Alex Eala's Excellence as Filipino Tennis Star
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. praised Filipina tennis star Alex Eala during a meeting at Malacañang, highlighting her historic achievements in international tennis. Eala, who recently reac...

item.title: Edo Governor Supports South Africa Returnees with N1m Aid and Resettlement Plans
item.description: The governor of Edo State, Godswill Akpabio, has announced a financial aid package of N1 million each for Nigerian citizens returning from South Africa. This initiative aims to support returnees with ...

item.title: Pizza Chain to Close 50 Underperforming Stores After Significant Losses
item.description: MTY Group, the parent company of Cold Stone Creamery and Wetzel’s Pretzels, has announced plans to close 68 corporate-owned restaurants over the next nine months. Of these, 45 to 50 underperforming Pa...

item.title: General Fusion becomes first publicly traded fusion company after Nasdaq debut
item.description: General Fusion, a fusion power company, began trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker GFUZ, marking its debut as the first publicly listed fusion energy firm. The stock surged 40% in its first day of t...

item.title: Former Durban Mayor Zandile Gumede Joins MK Party
item.description: Zandile Gumede, the former eThekwini mayor and ANC eThekwini chairperson, has officially transitioned to the uMkhonto weSizwe (MK) Party, where she has been appointed as deputy provincial convener in ...

item.title: President Samia urges caution against overuse of her name in government projects
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has advised public institutions to avoid excessive use of her name in naming government programs and projects. She highlighted that attaching 'Mama Samia' to multiple ini...

item.title: INEC Denies ADC Faction's Claim Of Uploading 2027 Candidates
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has officially denied claims by Nafiu Bala, factional leader of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), that his group uploaded candidates for the 2...

item.title: Lindsey Graham's Sister Appointed to Fill Vacant South Carolina Senate Seat
item.description: South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster announced on Monday that Darline Graham Nordone, the sister of late Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC), has been appointed to fill the vacant Senate seat left by her ...

item.title: Racial stereotypes in football and their roots in structural inequality
item.description: The article explores how racial bias in football persists through structural issues and stereotypes about Black athletes. It highlights how African players are often seen as naturally talented but lac...

item.title: Repeat Impaired Driving Charges Against Kitchener Man Highlight Recidivism Concerns
item.description: A man from Kitchener has been charged with impaired driving for the third time within less than a month, raising concerns about repeat offenses and public safety. The incident underscores the challeng...
